steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The task is to begin a narrative about Lena Oberon's life. A narrative opening must establish who the subject is and where and when her story starts — not jump ahead to achievements or episodes.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Lena Oberon's story begins in Mumbai (then known as Bombay), India, in 1911.: gives Mumbai (then Bombay) and 1911 — the biographical starting point a narrative opening requires. ✓
  • Choice B — Lena Oberon appeared in many films, including *Aren't We All?* (1932) and *Affectionately Yours* (1941), and was the first Indian-born actress to be nominated for an Academy Award.: skips ahead to career highlights without establishing an origin. ✗
  • Choice C — In 1941, Lena Oberon played the role of Sue Mayberry in the film *Affectionately Yours*.: drops into one moment mid-story rather than opening the narrative. ✗
  • Choice D — Though she would go on to receive credit for many films, Lena Oberon also played nameless, uncredited roles in many films such as the movie *Aren't We All?* (1932).: sketches a professional arc without grounding the reader in a narrative beginning. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
